message:
feat(fuzz): fuzz_runtime_execute_bytes + execute_bytes↔execute_frame parity test

Adds a cargo-fuzz target that drives `Runtime::execute_bytes` with
raw and structured inputs, and an internal metamorphic unit test
asserting that `execute_bytes` produces the same wire reply sequence
as `execute_frame` for multi-reply commands like SUBSCRIBE.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_runtime_execute_bytes.rs (new)
  * Mode-selector header byte routes the fuzz input to one of two
    paths:
      - raw-bytes: slam an arbitrary byte buffer at
        Runtime::execute_bytes capped at MAX_RAW_LEN=8_192 to catch
        panics in the protocol-parser → dispatcher seam without
        relying on well-formed RESP.
      - structured: interpret the remainder as an
        `arbitrary::Arbitrary`-derived `StructuredCommand { argv,
        now_offset_ms: u16 }` with MAX_ARGC=32 / MAX_ARG_LEN=256
        bounds. Encodes argv as a single RESP array frame and
        compares execute_bytes(raw) vs. parse(execute_frame(argv))
        reply-for-reply, catching any drift between the two
        entry points.
  * Host cap: libfuzzer wrapper returns early when input > 16_384
    bytes to bound per-iteration cost.
  * NOW_MS=1_000 + `now_offset_ms: u16` gives libfuzzer a 65 s
    timestamp space without unbounded u64 mutations.

crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs
  * New `parse_all_resp_frames(bytes) -> Vec<RespFrame>` test helper
    that walks a concatenated byte buffer via `parse_frame`,
    asserting each call consumes > 0 bytes (so a malformed reply
    fails fast instead of infinite-looping).
  * New `#[test] execute_bytes_matches_execute_frame_for_sequence_replies`:
    runs SUBSCRIBE alpha beta through both entry points on identical
    default-strict runtimes and asserts the full reply sequence
    compares equal frame-for-frame. This is the property the fuzz
    target then generalizes to arbitrary commands.

fuzz/Cargo.toml
  * Register `[[bin]] fuzz_runtime_execute_bytes` with the new
    path + no-test/doc/bench binding.

fuzz/README.md
  * New target row: "fuzz_runtime_execute_bytes — Runtime::execute_bytes
    driver — Structure-aware + raw RESP fuzz with execute_frame
    parity oracle."

fuzz/corpus/fuzz_runtime_execute_bytes/ (new)
  * Starter seeds so libfuzzer has coverage from iteration one:
      invalid_bulk.resp     (6 B)   — malformed bulk length prefix
      ping.resp             (15 B)  — minimal PING
      subscribe_multi.resp  (41 B)  — SUBSCRIBE alpha beta
                                       (three-reply sequence)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -84,6 +84,13 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_runtime_execute_bytes"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_runtime_execute_bytes.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_function_restore"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_function_restore.rs"

@@ -12,6 +12,7 @@ This directory contains fuzz targets for security-critical parser surfaces.
 | `fuzz_rdb_decoder` | RDB file parsing | Crash detector |
 | `fuzz_dump_restore` | DUMP/RESTORE payload handling | Structure-aware round-trip + hostile payload invariants |
 | `fuzz_acl_rules` | ACL file parsing and canonicalization | Structure-aware round-trip + hostile text stabilization |
+| `fuzz_runtime_execute_bytes` | Raw runtime RESP ingress | Structure-aware execute_bytes vs execute_frame differential |
 | `fuzz_function_restore` | FUNCTION LOAD/DUMP/RESTORE handling | Structure-aware round-trip + hostile restore atomicity |
 
 ## Running Fuzz Tests
@@ -35,6 +36,9 @@ cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_dump_restore
 # Run ACL rule parser fuzzer
 cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_acl_rules
 
+# Run raw runtime ingress fuzzer
+cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_runtime_execute_bytes
+
 # Run FUNCTION load/restore fuzzer
 cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_function_restore
